title: Tom Lee's Small-Cap Granny Shots ETF Reloads: First Solar, Zoom Among 10 New Buys
author: Chandrima Sanyal
description: Fundstrat Capital completed its May 2026 quarterly rebalance of the Fundstrat Granny Shots US Small- & Mid-Cap ETF (GRNJ), adding 10 stocks including First Solar, Zoom, Credo Technology, and Neurocrine Biosciences, while removing 8 positions. CIO Tom Lee positions the portfolio for a 'challenging but ultimately constructive' environment, with the strategy focusing on companies benefiting from structural tailwinds across themes like Millennials, Global Labor Supply, Energy, Cybersecurity, and Easing Financial Conditions.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Active rebalancing with strategic additions of quality companies across growth sectors; positioned for constructive market environment despite near-term challenges
Added to portfolio in rebalance, indicating alignment with renewable energy structural tailwinds
New addition to portfolio, likely benefiting from cybersecurity and technology themes
Added to portfolio, indicating healthcare sector positioning aligned with fund themes
Removed from portfolio as it no longer met thematic criteria
Removed from portfolio, indicating it no longer aligns with fund's investment themes

title: Carrier Global vs. Owens Corning: Which Industrials Stock Is a Better Buy in 2026?
author: Brendan Coffey
description: Carrier Global and Owens Corning are compared as infrastructure investment options. Carrier Global is pivoting toward climate solutions with strong AI data center demand (500%+ growth), while Owens Corning faces headwinds from construction cyclicality and a recent net loss. Carrier trades at a forward P/E of 24x (below sector average of 30.4x), while Owens Corning is cheaper at 12.5x but struggles with weaker residential demand and customer concentration risks.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Strong growth in AI data center business (500%+ increase), expanding heat pump demand in Europe, strategic pivot to higher-margin climate solutions, and valuation below sector average despite revenue decline. Management expects AI sector to contribute over $1.5 billion in revenue.

Reported net loss of $522 million in FY 2025 with negative 5.2% net margin, revenue declined 8%, high customer concentration risk (two customers represent 28% of sales), exposure to construction cyclicality, and litigation risks from fire safety issues with Paroc subsidiary.
Mentioned as a major competitor to Carrier Global in the HVAC and climate solutions market, but no specific performance data or analysis provided in the article.
Mentioned as a competitor to Carrier Global, but no specific performance analysis provided. The Motley Fool has positions in this company.
Mentioned as a competitor to Owens Corning in building materials, but no specific performance data or analysis provided in the article.

title: Carrier Board of Directors Declares Quarterly Cash Dividend
author: Prnewswire
description: Carrier Global Corporation announced that its Board of Directors declared a quarterly cash dividend of $0.24 per share, payable on August 10, 2026 to shareholders of record as of July 21, 2026.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The declaration of a quarterly dividend demonstrates the company's financial strength and commitment to returning capital to shareholders. Regular dividend payments are typically viewed positively by investors as they indicate stable cash flows and management confidence in the company's financial position.

title: AirJoule Technologies Announces $15.0 Million Registered Direct Offering of Common Stock
author: Na
description: AirJoule Technologies (NASDAQ: AIRJ) announced a registered direct offering of 3.66 million shares for approximately $15 million in gross proceeds. Combined with existing cash, the net proceeds are expected to fully fund the company through 2028 and support commercialization of its AirJoule Core and Prime systems. The offering reflects institutional investor confidence in the company's commercialization roadmap.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The company successfully secured $15 million in funding from institutional investors, demonstrating market confidence. The capital raise extends runway to 2028 and supports advancement of commercial products (AirJoule Core and Prime systems). CEO commentary highlights progress on commercialization roadmap and market opportunities.

The company successfully secured $15 million in funding from institutional investors, demonstrating market confidence. title: HVAC Market Opportunity Report 2026: Surge in Urban Development, Energy Efficiency, and Smart Technologies Drive Expansion - Growth Analysis and Forecasts 2025-2035
author: Researchandmarkets.Com
description: The global HVAC market is projected to grow from USD 524.9 billion in 2025 to USD 1.2 trillion by 2035, driven by rapid urbanization, energy efficiency demands, and smart technology adoption. The air conditioning equipment segment leads with USD 215.8 billion in 2025, while the indirect distribution channel dominates with 74% market share. The U.S. market alone is valued at USD 156.9 billion and expected to grow at 10.2% CAGR.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Major HVAC manufacturer benefiting from increased demand for energy-efficient cooling solutions and smart HVAC technologies in expanding markets.
Major HVAC manufacturer well-positioned in a market expected to reach USD 1.2 trillion by 2035 with strong growth in energy-efficient systems.
Major HVAC manufacturer positioned to benefit from strong market growth at 8.1% CAGR and increasing demand for advanced environmental control systems.
